channel.title: EnergyPricesToday — Live Oil Prices, Gas Prices, Energy Geopolitics
channel.description: Daily updates on oil prices, U.S. gas prices, electricity rates, and energy geopolitics.

item.title: WTI Tops $100, Brent $111 on Iran Hormuz Proposal Uncertainty
item.description: WTI crude futures jumped more than 3% Tuesday April 28 to settle above $99.93, with Brent above $111.26 as markets weighed Iran's new Hormuz proposal and Trump's skepticism. The 7th consecutive session of gains pushed both benchmarks to highs not seen since early April.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: U.S. Gas Average Hits $4.14, Highest in Nearly Four Years
item.description: AAA reported Tuesday April 28 that the U.S. national average for regular gasoline rose to $4.144 per gallon, the highest level in nearly four years. Diesel reached $5.689. Crude oil's seven-session rally to $99.93 WTI is feeding through to retail prices.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Trump Says Iran in ’State of Collapse’ as Hormuz Standoff Drags Into Ninth Week
item.description: President Trump posted Tuesday April 28 that Iran had informed the U.S. it was in 'a State of Collapse' and wanted Hormuz reopened. The post came as the U.S. weighs Iran's Monday proposal to lift the blockade in exchange for reopening the strait.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Iran Proposes Hormuz Reopening if U.S. Lifts Naval Blockade
item.description: Iran submitted a formal proposal Monday April 27 to reopen the Strait of Hormuz if the U.S. lifts its naval blockade and ends military operations, while postponing nuclear talks for a later phase. Trump and his national security team discussed the offer; early indications suggest the proposal is unlikely to be accepted in its current form.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Araghchi Leaves Pakistan Without Meeting U.S. Officials as Talks Collapse
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Araghchi left Islamabad Sunday April 26 without meeting any U.S. officials, after Trump cancelled the Witkoff-Kushner trip. Pakistani mediation continues but direct talks remain stalled.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Trump Cancels Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan Trip as Iran Talks Stall
item.description: Trump cancelled the planned trip of U.S. envoys Witkoff and Kushner to Islamabad on Saturday April 25, halting the second round of U.S.-Iran peace talks before it could begin.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: WTI Surges 13% on Week as Hormuz Stays Closed and Talks Stall
item.description: WTI crude rose 13% on the week and Brent rose 18%, the biggest weekly gains since early March, as Hormuz stayed closed and U.S.-Iran diplomacy deadlocked. WTI settled at $94.7, Brent at $104.4.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Israeli Strikes Kill Lebanese Journalist Amal Khalil During Extended Ceasefire
item.description: Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil was killed and others wounded in an Israeli strike April 22, sharply straining the extended Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. Multiple new strikes followed across south Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Trump Orders Navy to 'Shoot and Kill' Iranian Mine-Laying Vessels in Strait
item.description: President Trump ordered the U.S. Navy to 'shoot and kill' Iranian small boats laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, marking a sharp escalation in U.S. enforcement posture.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: U.S. Boards Supertanker Carrying Iranian Oil in Indian Ocean
item.description: U.S. forces boarded a supertanker carrying Iranian oil in the Indian Ocean April 22, signaling that interdiction operations now extend well beyond the Persian Gulf and naval blockade zone.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Saudi Aramco East-West Pipeline Continues at Full Capacity Through Blockade
item.description: Saudi Aramco's East-West Pipeline continues operating at full 5 million bpd capacity, bypassing Hormuz and delivering crude to Red Sea port of Yanbu for export.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Trump Extends Iran Ceasefire Indefinitely, Citing 'Seriously Fractured' Iranian Government
item.description: Trump extended the U.S.-Iran ceasefire indefinitely via Truth Social on April 21, citing a 'seriously fractured' Iranian government at the request of Pakistan.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Brent Touches $101 Intraday Before Retreating on Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Brent briefly broke $100 on Vance-no-travel reports before retreating on Trump's ceasefire extension. WTI +3% at $92.13, Brent +3% at $98.48.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Iran FM Araghchi Calls U.S. Naval Blockade 'An Act of War' and Ceasefire Violation
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi denounced the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports as 'an act of war' and a clear violation of the ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Hezbollah Fires Rockets at Israeli Troops in Southern Lebanon, Violating Ceasefire
item.description: Israeli military says Hezbollah fired rockets at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon Tuesday, breaching the 10-day ceasefire ahead of U.S.-mediated talks.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Maersk, Hapag-Lloyd Maintain Hormuz Transit Suspension Amid Continued Uncertainty
item.description: Global shipping giants Maersk and Hapag-Lloyd are maintaining their Hormuz transit suspensions despite the ceasefire extension. Vessels continuing to route around Africa.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Explained: Timeline, Terms, and Extension
item.description: Complete timeline of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire: how it began, the key terms, Trump's April 21 indefinite extension, and what markets expect next.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: U.S. Naval Blockade of Iranian Ports Remains in Place Despite Ceasefire Extension
item.description: Trump's ceasefire extension keeps the U.S. naval blockade of Iranian ports in place. 28 ships have been turned back. Iran calls blockade an 'act of war.'
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Cheniere Energy, Venture Global Ride LNG Premium as Qatar Flows Still Disrupted
item.description: U.S. LNG exporters Cheniere and Venture Global benefit from continued Qatari LNG disruption through Hormuz. Terminals running near 14 Bcf/d capacity.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: IRGC Threatens to Target Oil Facilities in Neighboring Countries Hosting U.S. Attacks
item.description: Iran's Revolutionary Guard warned it would target oil facilities in neighboring countries that allow U.S. attacks from their territory. Gulf producers on high alert.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Energy Stocks Mixed as Ceasefire Extension Eases but Blockade Stays
item.description: Energy sector stocks traded mixed Tuesday as Trump's ceasefire extension eased geopolitical tension but the continuing blockade kept supply-disruption premiums elevated.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Vance's Islamabad Trip Put on Hold as Iran Fails to Confirm Delegation
item.description: VP Vance's planned Islamabad trip for U.S.-Iran peace talks was put on hold Tuesday after Iran failed to confirm a delegation. Talks remain possible at 'a moment's notice.'
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: OPEC+ April Output Increase Proceeding Despite Market Volatility
item.description: OPEC+ is proceeding with its 206,000 bpd April output increase despite Iran conflict volatility. No emergency meeting called. Gulf producers monitor IRGC threats.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Cheniere Energy Sees Q1 LNG Demand Surge Before Strait Reopens — EnergyPricesToday.com
item.description: Cheniere Energy, the largest U.S. LNG exporter, is expected to report strong Q1 LNG demand benefiting from the Strait of Hormuz disruption that pushed...
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Iran Blames U.S. for Failure of 21-Hour Islamabad Negotiations — EnergyPricesToday.com
item.description: Iran Blames U.S. for Failure of 21-Hour Islamabad Negotiations — breaking energy market analysis from EnergyPricesToday.com
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: European Refiners Boost Margins With Russian Crude Alternatives — EnergyPricesToday.com
item.description: European Refiners Boost Margins With Russian Crude Alternatives — energy market analysis from EnergyPricesToday.com
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: NuScale Small Modular Reactor Receives Full NRC Certification — EnergyPricesToday.com
item.description: The 77 MWe module becomes the first SMR to complete U.S. regulatory approval, clearing the path for deployment.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Diesel and Heating Oil Margins Diverge as Trucking Demand Holds — EnergyPricesToday.com
item.description: Diesel and Heating Oil Margins Diverge as Trucking Demand Holds. Fundamental supply-demand balance remains the core driver beneath the geopolitical noise.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: European TTF Gas Rallies 12 Percent on Cold Weather Forecast — EnergyPricesToday.com
item.description: A late-season cold snap across Northern Europe draws down storage reserves faster than seasonal norms.
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff

item.title: Gulf of Mexico Rig Count Rises to 13 as Deepwater Investment Returns — EnergyPricesToday.com
item.description: Gulf of Mexico Rig Count Rises to 13 as Deepwater Investment Returns — rig count analysis from EnergyPricesToday.com
item.dc:creator: EnergyPricesToday Staff
